owner: Runtime / engine team with Daxis SRE, storage, catalog, gateway, and web platform owners
repoOwnedScope: Axon-owned metrics, fallback vocabulary, contract fixtures, release evidence commands, and operational templates for Daxis integration.
externalProductionScope: Daxis-owned production dashboards, alert routing, oncall schedules, rollout configuration services, production table inventories, audit pipelines, and service incident playbooks.

dashboards.name: browser_execution
dashboards.purpose: Show where Daxis executes interactive reads and whether Axon browser execution is the selected path.

dashboards.name: fallback_and_block_reasons
dashboards.purpose: Track deterministic server fallback, policy block, and unsupported-feature reasons without losing the original routing decision.

dashboards.name: data_fetch_and_object_access
dashboards.purpose: Correlate browser bytes and object-grant reads with storage broker authorization evidence.

dashboards.name: worker_health
dashboards.purpose: Expose browser worker startup, cancellation, terminal error, and artifact drift signals before rollout widens.

runbooks.name: resolver_failure
symptoms: descriptor resolver returns invalid_table_uri or snapshot_version_not_found
firstChecks: Confirm request_id and correlation_id are present across UI, gateway, catalog, and resolver logs.
Check catalog lookup, policy decision, and pinned snapshot resolution for the affected table.
Verify resolver responses still match the Daxis descriptor fixtures and schema hash manifest.
Confirm Axon worker commands do not contain credential profile identifiers or table-root credentials.
runbooks.rollback: Route affected tenants or table classes to server_fallback while preserving the resolver failure reason.

runbooks.name: object_grant_failure
symptoms: object access audit event missing grant, table, or query identity
firstChecks: Confirm grant expiry, route capability flags, and grant-scoped path validation.
Check object grant audit events for tenantId, workspaceId, tableId, grantId, requestId, correlationId, and axonQueryId.
Verify signed URL or proxy range responses are object-scoped and do not broaden table-root access.
Run the Axon object-grant SDK and contract artifact gates before widening rollout.
runbooks.rollback: Disable brokered_delta for the affected rollout segment and return read-access plans as sql_fallback_required.

runbooks.name: cors_failure
runbooks.owner: Daxis storage platform and security engineering
symptoms: range reads fail with storage_cors_failed or object_store_protocol
browser cannot read signed XML endpoint responses
preflight headers differ from the production endpoint shape
firstChecks: Validate the exact production XML endpoint path used by signed URLs, not a localhost or JSON API substitute.
Confirm Access-Control-Allow-Origin, allowed methods, exposed headers, and range-read behavior for the Daxis app origin.
Check whether proxy mode is required for the tenant, browser, or storage provider.
Verify failing URLs are redacted in UI events, logs, and error diagnostics.
runbooks.rollback: Switch the affected access mode to proxy or server_fallback until production CORS evidence is restored.

runbooks.name: worker_startup_failure
runbooks.owner: Runtime / engine team and Daxis web platform
symptoms: WASM artifact or bundle selection drifts
browser matrix starts failing for supported Daxis flows
firstChecks: Check worker artifact identity, runtimeSku, selected bundle tier, and browser feature probes.
Run the browser worker matrix for descriptor open/query/cancel and unsupported-feature paths.
Run dependency guardrails and worker artifact size reports for the legacy narrow and DataFusion default paths.
Compare startup and memory baselines against the latest release evidence bundle.
runbooks.rollback: Disable browser execution for affected browser families or release channels and route to server_fallback.

runbooks.name: elevated_fallback_rates
runbooks.owner: Daxis query platform and runtime / engine team
symptoms: browser fallback frequency rises above the rollout threshold
server compute usage increases for tables expected to run in browser
firstChecks: Break down fallback_reason by tenantId, workspaceId, tableClass, runtimeSku, table features, and query shape.
Compare affected SQL classes against the Daxis query corpus and browser DataFusion parity record.
Check recent rollout changes, artifact identity, dependency changes, and compatibility dashboard drift.
Confirm Daxis preserves the original fallback or block reason when routing to server execution.
runbooks.rollback: Narrow rollout to the last stable tenant, workspace, or table-class segment and keep server fallback enabled.

rolloutControls.killSwitch: A Daxis-owned control must force server_fallback by tenant, workspace, table class, browser family, and runtime SKU without requiring a new Axon release.

compatibilityDashboard.owner: Daxis catalog platform and QA / performance engineering

verificationCommands: cargo test -p query-router -p native-query-runtime -p delta-control-plane -p wasm-query-runtime -p wasm-query-session -p wasm-http-object-store -p wasm-parquet-engine -p wasm-delta-snapshot -p browser-sdk -p browser-engine-worker

cargo check -p wasm-http-object-store -p wasm-parquet-engine -p wasm-delta-snapshot -p wasm-query-runtime -p wasm-query-session -p browser-sdk -p browser-engine-worker --target wasm32-unknown-unknown --locked
npm exec -- playwright test --config=playwright.config.ts --grep "Daxis descriptor-resolver|preserves cancellation errors|surfaces unsupported feature errors"

stableDefaultBlockers: Production dashboard URLs and alert ownership are absent from this repository.
Production oncall schedules and service incident playbooks are absent from this repository.
Tenant and workspace rollout controls are Daxis service work outside this repository.
Production table compatibility inventory is Daxis catalog and QA work outside this repository.
External proof artifacts listed in docs/release-gates/daxis-external-proof-packet.json are Daxis-owned and absent from this repository.
